_doc: AUTHORED sidecar: the human meaning of each gate flag (group:index) used by BOTH the enemy (spawn_control) and item (item_control) layers. This is the ONLY hand-edited input to the trigger-summary layer; everything else in room-data.json's summary/logic/_flag_glossary is DERIVED from the decoded control blocks + these labels by scripts/trigger_decode.py. 'label' is a short event-noun used verbatim in summaries; 'event_room' is the room whose script sets the flag (informational; the primary 0x25 setter). Adding a new gated room: run `scripts/trigger_decode.py --check`; if it reports an unlabeled flag, add it here.
_basis: Covers BOTH the enemy gates (spawn_control) and the item gates (item_control). Enemy group-0 flags 0:9/0:15/0:35/0:58/0:63 are CE/doc/notepad-confirmed (docs/dc1/ROOM-SPAWN-CONDITIONS.md). The rest are named from the static self-latch pattern (the room's own event sub sets the flag that gates its placement) plus the 0x25 setter trace; narrative names are INFERRED, not byte-proven. The Backup-Generator cluster (0:44/0:45/0:47/0:51) is corroborated at the area level by the beta-list notepad (the 'Passaggio Gen. di Riserva' dinosaur whose fate gates later scenes). Flags with no 0x25 setter (e.g. 0:0/0:1/0:5/0:6 and the group-7 item flags) are engine/native/pickup-set; their narrative is unverified. ITEM flag banks: group 7 = engine/pickup-set item-placement flags (no script setter; via 0x26), group 11 = key/item-held (via the 0x52 test op), group 2 = scenario-segment state, plus puzzle-state group-0 flags. Item gate STRUCTURE is byte-decoded (confirmed); the flag NAMES are inferred at the same grade as the enemy ones.
